name: top
description: Display and manage Linux processes in real-time with an interactive full-screen interface

globalOptions.name: -b
globalOptions.description: Batch mode - send output to stdout or file (useful for scripting, no interactive input)

globalOptions.name: -c
globalOptions.description: Toggle full command line display vs. program name only

globalOptions.name: -d
globalOptions.description: Delay time interval in seconds between screen updates (supports fractional seconds)

globalOptions.name: -H
globalOptions.description: Show individual threads instead of aggregating by process

globalOptions.name: -i
globalOptions.description: Toggle idle process display - when set, tasks that have not used any CPU since the last update are hidden

globalOptions.name: -n
globalOptions.description: Maximum number of iterations (refreshes) before exiting

globalOptions.name: -o
globalOptions.description: Sort by specified field name (e.g., %CPU, %MEM, TIME+, RES, PID)

globalOptions.name: -O
globalOptions.description: Print all available sort field names and exit

globalOptions.name: -p
globalOptions.description: Monitor only processes with specified PIDs (comma-separated, max 20)

globalOptions.name: -u
globalOptions.description: Show only processes owned by the specified user (name or UID)

globalOptions.name: -U
globalOptions.description: Show only processes owned by the specified user (matches real, effective, saved, filesystem UID)
Toggle single/separate CPU states display (show per-CPU stats)

globalOptions.name: -E
globalOptions.description: Scale summary area memory units (k, m, g, t, p, e)

globalOptions.name: -e
globalOptions.description: Scale task area memory units (k, m, g, t, p, e)

globalOptions.name: -w
globalOptions.description: Set output width (in batch mode); without value uses 512 columns

globalOptions.name: -S
globalOptions.description: Toggle cumulative time mode (include dead child process time)

examples.description: Batch mode with single iteration (for scripting)
Show only processes owned by www-data
Capture 5 iterations at 2s intervals to a file
Show threads for a specific process
Show full command lines in batch mode
